reasoning: The market consensus traded at 0.36 on October 16, 2025. Given the strict resolution criteria (no human backup, no observers, non-tester valid rides > 400m, and specifically reaching a cumulative 10,000 rides by the end of 2025), there is substantial risk of Tesla missing this milestone. Elon Musk sets famously optimistic timelines, and a launch happening this late in the year leaves very little room to reach 10,000 rides before January 1, 2026, assuming operations have not fully scaled yet. A slight downward adjustment from the 0.36 market anchoring makes sense due to both time decay (10 fewer days between Oct 16 and Oct 26) and typical Manifold retail bias towards optimistic Tesla timelines. I forecast 0.29.
